examples: Young people who are digital natives are indeed becoming more skillful at separating fact from fiction in cyberspace.
Securing cyberspace is hard because the architecture of the internet was designed to promote connectivity, not security.
As the Internet plays an increasingly important role in a country, attacks on a country's cyberspace might have extremely negative effects on it.
The big chains' distribution systems, so effective in stocking superstores in malls, are of little advantage in cyberspace.
Inevitably, given the pervasiveness of information technology, cyberspace is also becoming a question of security.
